title: PB: AI Focus Relevance Threshold config field (v2.176.1)
author: adsvisory
body:
## Summary

Companion to **avonetiq/avq-karsa#76** (PB focus anchoring + multilingual fidelity fix, v2.125.0).

Adds a "Focus Relevance Gate" section on `Configure → PB Engine → AI Config` editing the new `pb.ai.relevance_threshold` setting (default `0.50`, range 0–1). Configures the new fail-open focus-relevance gate on AI-generated prompts in Karsa.

## Why

Karsa now applies a Jina similarity gate to AI-generated prompts (catches LLM drift to generic, category-wide questions despite the FOCUS ANCHORING rule in the system prompt). Per the zero-hardcode rule, every setting must be editable in Shastra.

## Why a separate threshold from `pb.external.relevance_threshold`

- **AI gate** default `0.50` — fail-open (Jina outage keeps all AI prompts, since they're the bulk of every book).
- **External gate** default `0.45` — fail-closed (Jina outage drops all external SERP/PAA/autocomplete, since they're supplementary).
- AI prompts are seeded with the focus and should be more on-topic, hence the slightly higher default.

After a few builds, review per-language rejection rates and tune: too many valid on-focus rejected → lower toward 0.45; too many generic prompts slipping through → raise toward 0.55.

## Changes

- `src/components/configure/pb-engine/PBAIConfigTab.tsx` — new `<Card>` with `ConfigSectionLabel` "Focus Relevance Gate" + `ConfigNumberInput` for `pb.ai.relevance_threshold`. Placed between Provider/Model and Token Limits.

## Validation

- `tsc --noEmit` clean.
- No new components or hooks — uses existing `ConfigNumberInput` and `useConfigWorkspace` patterns.
- Uses existing `Card` + `ConfigSectionLabel` layout consistent with sibling tabs.

## Test plan

- [ ] Open `Configure → PB Engine → AI Config` → confirm "Focus Relevance Gate" section appears
- [ ] Edit the value → confirm Save button activates and persists to `pb.ai.relevance_threshold` in Karsa settings
- [ ] Reload the page → confirm the edited value is shown (round-trip via `GET /api/settings`)
- [ ] Confirm the field is gated 0–1 (out-of-range values rejected)
- [ ] Trigger a PB build after editing → confirm Karsa's pipeline picks up the new threshold (visible in "AI relevance gate" activity log line)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commits.message: PB: AI Focus Relevance Threshold config field (v2.176.1)

title: Isolate job error/activity logs to the job (v2.176.1)
author: optimize-avo
body:
The Job Inspector "Errors" and "Activity" tabs leaked global logs from other jobs and domains, and rendered "Invalid Date" for system entries.

Root cause: useJobLogs called GET /api/logs?traceId=<jobId>, but Karsa's logs query schema expects snake_case `trace_id`. Zod stripped the unknown `traceId` key, so no trace filter was applied and the endpoint returned the latest 200 logs across ALL jobs/domains. That same endpoint also returns snake_case fields (created_at), so `new Date(log.createdAt)` was NaN -> "Invalid Date".

Fix:
- Switch useJobLogs to the trace-scoped endpoint GET /api/logs/trace/:jobId (path param parsed server-side), so logs are isolated to this job. This endpoint also returns camelCase fields (createdAt/traceId) via formatLog, fixing the timestamps.
- Guard timestamp rendering in JobInspector (mergeActivityItems + formatLogTime helper) so any missing/bad time shows "—" instead of "Invalid Date".

commits.message: fix(inspect): isolate job error/activity logs to the job (v2.176.1)

title: Make response schemas documentation-only — stop FST_ERR_RE…
author: ikhsan-sr
body:
…SPONSE_SERIALIZATION — v0.46.4

Staging hit HTTP 500 (FST_ERR_RESPONSE_SERIALIZATION, "Response doesn't match the schema") because the zod serializer ran `schema.parse()` on every response and threw whenever a handler's real payload didn't exactly match its documented response schema.

Fix: response schemas are now DOCUMENTATION-ONLY. The serializer returns the raw payload (like Fastify's default for un-schema'd routes) — it never strips fields and never 500s on a mismatch. Request validation is unchanged (handlers rely on its coercion/defaults). Docs output is unaffected (docs:check still 371 ops).

This restores pre-PR response behaviour for every endpoint while keeping the docs.

commits.message: fix(docs): make response schemas documentation-only — stop FST_ERR_RESPONSE_SERIALIZATION — v0.46.4

title: Add customRules to bypass Better Auth per-endpoint rate l…
author: ikhsan-sr
body:
…imits

Better Auth applies stricter per-endpoint defaults (e.g. /sign-in/email defaults to ~3 attempts per 10s per identifier) that override the global `max` set in the rateLimit config. Without customRules, bumping AUTH_RATE_LIMIT_MAX has no effect on the endpoints that actually need it during load testing.

Endpoints covered:
- /sign-in/email       (was ~3/10s, now 60/min default, env-overridable)
- /sign-up/email       (was ~3/10s, now 30/min default)
- /forget-password     (was ~3/10s, now 20/min default)
- /get-session         (high-volume, 2000/min default)
- /organization/set-active (200/min default)

When AUTH_RATE_LIMIT_MAX env is set (e.g. 5000 for load test window), all per-endpoint limits inherit that value, allowing realistic load testing without 429 cascade.

target: v0.X.X (PATCH bump on merge — additive, no schema change)

title: Competitor domain no longer force-reset to own domain on select
author: optimize-avo
body:
useActiveDomain auto-select guard was checking existsAsOwned (ownDomains only), which excludes all signal/competitor domains. Any time a competitor domain was selected, the effect immediately reset selectedDomainId back to the primary own domain — making it impossible to view or trigger AS for a competitor.

Fix: check existsInAll (allDomains) instead. Only reset to own domain if the selected domain no longer exists in the org at all.

commits.message: fix: competitor domain no longer force-reset to own domain on select

title: Use absolute callbackURL for Google OAuth redirect (v0.14.2)
author: davidsurosegoro
body: Google OAuth was redirecting to localhost:3001 (Sutra) instead of localhost:5175 (Astra) after login because callbackURL was a relative path. Fixed to use window.location.origin + from, matching magic link.
